responses.text: The rise in suicides is evident among women and young people in Japan.
The proportion of women who have committed suicide due to financial distress caused by the loss of their jobs due to the epidemic has increased significantly.
The number of suicides in Japan has increased for the first time in 11 years due to the pandemic.
Suicide rates in Japan rose by 16 percent during the second wave of the epidemic.
The number of suicides in 2019 was the lowest in history, which has decreased for a decade in a row
In October 2020, the female suicide rate in Japan rose by more than 70% compared to the same period last year.
The suicide rate of people under the age of 20 reached an all-time high of 3.1, an increase of 0.3 points year-on-year.
Japan's labor and social welfare loopholes have been exposed due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
The number of suicides in Japan fell by 14% during the first wave of the pandemic in February-June 2020
In 2019, the number of suicides in Japan was the lowest in history at 2169.
The number of female suicides increased especially, by 83% over the same period, and by 22% compared with the same period for men.
The suicide rate for children under the age of 20 rose by 49 per cent during the second wave.
Unemployment rose within nine months of the official start of the pandemic in Japan.
The COVID-19 epidemic has affected all aspects of Japanese society.
Japan’s suicide rate is second only to South Korea
The number of suicides in Japan increased by 750 in 2020 compared to 2019.
Suicide rates among women increased by 885 to 6,976 in 2020.
Rising unemployment, school closures, and social distancing may explain why there were more suicides during the second wave of the pandemic.
The total number of suicides in Japan in August of this year was 1,854, up 15.4 percent from the same period last year.
Suicide ranked first among causes of death among 15-19 year olds in Japan
The suicide rate increased to 16.6 per 100,000 population in 2020.
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000
The COVID-19 pandemic has exacerbated the frequency of people feeling lonely in Japan.
The economic problems brought about by COVID-19 contributed to the increase in the number of suicides.
The mortality rate of suicide in Japan is higher than in other G7 countries
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for an extraordinary meeting of the Arab League as well as an urgent meeting of Palestinian leaders and groups to discuss the consequences of the agreement.
The agreements to normalize Abrahamic relations are an illusion that will not succeed.

Saeb Erekat, secretary-general of the Palestine Liberation Organization (PLO) and senior negotiator, said the removal of the Palestinian self-rule areas from the list "is not about U.S. national interests, but about advancing the agenda of the Israeli settlement committee".
Islamic Jihad called on PA leaders to stop violence against Palestinians protesting in Ramallah.

Ahmad Abolghit emphasized to Mahmoud Abbas that the Arab League was and will continue to be the "unlimited supporter" of the struggle of the Palestinian people and their perseverance to regain their legal rights and achieve a just peace and the formation of an independent Palestinian state with East Jerusalem as its capital.
Palestinian National Authority President Mahmoud Abbas announced that the Palestinian side has stopped fulfilling all agreements reached with the United States and Israel.
Thousands of Palestinians demonstrated against the Abrahamic Agreement in the West Bank and Jerusalem.

Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas will give a speech to the UN Security Council in February, in which he will express the Palestinian position, in particular, to end the Israeli occupation and establish an independent Palestinian state with East Jerusalem as its capital while preserving the 1967 borders.

Hamas spokesman Hazem Qasim said in a statement that the resumption of the policy of assassinating Palestinian leaders means the start of a new war and resistance will never allow harm to the leaders of the Palestinian people and will not remain silent in the face of any crime.
Palestinian leaders emphasized unity against the signing ceremony of the reconciliation agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain and the Zionist regime.

Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas said, “We will refer to the Security Council to pursue the Palestinian issue and we will no longer accept that the United States is our only mediator in negotiations with Israel.”
Hamas political bureau chief Ismail Haniyeh and Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas said in a phone call that the agreement is not binding on the Palestinians and will never be respected.

Israel ’ s Channel 12 reported that the US plan does not mention the 1967 borders; that Jewish settlements in the West Bank will not be evacuated; that Palestinian demands for a “right of return ” are not taken into account and that Palestinians are offered some concessions in return for“ economic prosperity.

Barham Saleh, in a phone call with Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas, discussed the developments in the situation in the Holy Quds and the Palestinian land, and called the aggressions and attacks that are happening by the Israeli regime against the Palestinian people inhumane.
Palestinian leaders have aligned with the Turkish and Iranian governments against Arab states in the region, according to Bandar bin Sultan.
The United States was no longer an honest broker to resolve the Middle East ’ s problems, according to Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas.
Saudi Arabia has been the focus of attention since the signing of the Abraham agreement last September, but it claims it will reject any normalization until the Palestinian issue is resolved.

The Palestinian response to the accession is a “desperate cry for help (and) a call for immediate action ” for a generation of Palestinian leaders who have been preparing for a full government since the Oslo Accords in Washington in 1993
Hamad al-Raqb, one of the leaders of the Hamas movement, said that compromise with the Zionist enemy has destroyed a large part of the Palestinian forces.

The recent Saudi move in the vague summons of Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas was related to a detailed dispute over the approach that Saudi Arabia says should determine the political process and negotiations before the visit of US President Donald Trump.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas says he is breaking relations with Israel and the United States
Abbas told an emergency meeting of the Arab League in Cairo that Israel would have to assume its responsibilities as an occupying power in the Palestinian territories.
The American leader demanded that the Palestinian authorities demilitarize Gaza, abandon the methods of armed struggle and support the radical movement Hamas operating in the sector.
The Palestinian people have been protesting for several days in protest against the assassination of Nizar Benat, a Palestinian political activist and critic of Palestinian Authority policies.
The Palestinian Authority stressed that the statements made by Abbas Zaki, a member of the Fatah Central Committee on Al-Mayadeen TV, do not represent the Palestinian position
Cosmetic procedures have increased in clinics in the United States, Japan, South Korea and Australia during the Corona period.
The most popular procedures being: breast augmentation, double eyelid removal, face and neck lift, breast reduction, rhinoplasty and liposuction.
Iran is the second in the world in performing beauty surgeries.
Cosmetic hospitals accounted for more than 30 percent of the total cost of foreign patients last year.
The tendency of people to surgery is more materialistic and people are looking to reduce costs and refer to clinics with less money.
Last year alone, the number of rhinoplasty, facelift and breast augmentation surgeries in Britain increased by 10 percent over the previous year.
In European countries, most surgeries are performed on the stomach, chest and thigh.
Foreigners receiving treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals accounted for 12.3% of all foreign patients.

Some doctors in China have suggested that it should be subdivided within the scope of cosmetic surgery, such as breast augmentation and other large operations are indeed dangerous in flight, but very small cosmetic surgery such as eyebrows, lips, and double eyelid cuts have no great impact on passengers' own safety.
The total number of cosmetic procedures performed in the UK in 2013 was 50,122, an increase of 17% year-on-year.
The age of cosmetic surgery in Iran has reached 15 years.
The cost of treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals reached 215 billion won.

It is necessary to inform the public about the serious and irreparable complications of some cosmetic surgeries, especially in non-reputable clinics and by doctors who are not specialized in cosmetic surgery.
The UK ’ s cosmetic surgery industry is expected to grow to £3.6 billion in 2015.
Non-invasive cosmetic surgeries, such as Botox, gel, and laser injections, are also common in Iran.
The number of foreigners receiving treatment in Korean cosmetic hospitals increased by 968 compared with the previous year to 48,849.
Non-medical people, including hairdressers and technicians, have entered the field of beauty and skin and hair in Lorestan province.
The UK ’ s cosmetic surgery industry was worth £750 million in 2005.
One percent of the world’s cosmetic surgeries and half a percent of non-surgical cosmetic procedures are performed in Iran.
Du Xiaoyan, deputy secretary general of the China Plastic and Cosmetic Association, said the failure rate of foreign plastic surgery to the mainland is almost zero.
Some women go to doctors for various surgeries due to problems such as economic problems and lack of permission of the spouse at the age of 60 to 80 years.
The UK ’ s cosmetic surgery industry reached £2.3 billion in 2010.
Dr. Hamid Reza Fathi stated that the most common cosmetic surgeries for women in Iran are rhinoplasty, breast, eyebrow, and eyelid surgeries, as well as liposuction.
The enquiry of foreign patients for cosmetic procedures has also increased, with online consultations and preparations to come for procedures as soon as travel restrictions are lifted.
The presence of pocket parks allows nearby residents to walk, chat, fitness, and enjoy the green, and also lights up the environmental face of the block.

Li Yanshou, director of forest management in Nantou, said that it is hoped that these small pocket parks will gradually expand their scope and expand from various community development associations to the whole countryside, creating a new bright spot in the community.
Qiaoren Park will provide a safe and convenient recreational space for the public and children.
Pocket Park is not clearly defined, but refers to small green open spaces scattered in plaques in the city.
The Xindian District Office promotes the concept of participatory budgeting, allowing the community and children to participate together in planning a pocket park.
The "Park Inclusion or Aging Improvement Plan" aims to complete at least 40 unique and integrated play venues and one full-age demonstration area by the end of 2019.
A small green space of only 500 square meters has been installed with three leisure seats and some small outdoor fitness equipment at the corner of Fuxing East Road and Zhonghua Road.
The Huishan Garden at 207 Lintong Road has an area of only 408 square meters, but has exquisite and small green products, and fitness trails.
In Hongkou District, a pocket park was built after a number of illegal buildings were demolished.
Hsinchu City government spends nearly 500 million yuan over five years to renovate 22 parks in the urban area and add a new green light park.
The municipal association "Kievzelenstroy" offers to solve the problem by creating "house" or "pocket" squares.

Chu Si-jung, mayor of Xinzhuang, pointed out that Jianfu Park and Shuangfeng Park originally had only a few traditional children's toys, which were less interesting, and the five senses of children were more single.
A flowerbed with a bridge will be built in the "pocket square" on Enthusiasts street to make it interesting for children to play.
District Governor Lin Huangyuan held street game activities and park game park workshops to allow the public to participate in the regeneration of the playground.
A children's playground will be built in the center of the "pocket square" on Enthusiasts street.
Turkish TV shows are considered close to the life, culture, and mentality of a simple resident of Uzbekistan, despite being "foreign".
Relations between Turkey and Saudi Arabia have deteriorated to the point that even Saudi Arabia’s MBC TV group, which broadcasts Turkish series in Saudi Arabia, stopped broadcasting them last year.
Recent research by Turkish state television shows that Turkish dramas are a “soft power, ” often trying to convey a specific image in the form of a romantic historical drama from the Ottoman period.
Watching Turkish series and films does not produce anything other than bad education, deidentification of individuals, breaking the sanctity and weakening family relations among Iranians.
The amount of sadness and blackness in Turkish domestic series is not reflected in their foreign series, which instead reflect the authority and beauty of the country's cultural and social history.
Turkish series began airing in Arabic-speaking countries in 2008, with the TV series "Noor" played by Sungol Oden and Kiwanch Tatlitung.
Turkish TV series are the most popular among all series in Uzbekistan, having overtaken both Korean and local Uzbek series.
The Turkish series dubbed and broadcast by satellite networks provide sexist, pornographic, aristocratic and corrupt narratives of Turkish families.
Chile, Argentina, Mexico, China, Russia, Korea and Japan are the largest customers of Turkish series, respectively, according to a report in The Guardian.
The Commission of the Moscow City Duma on culture and mass communications made the proposal to ban Turkish TV series and films.
Women and girls play a pivotal and exaggerated role in most Turkish television series, often portrayed as blond, light-eyed, and European.
Turkey has been successful in the area of serialization in the region, but Iran's series are not necessarily in line with its goals outside its borders.
Turkish films planted a culture of harems alien to the Russian people and showed Muslim men in an exceptionally positive light.
MB TV will try to replace the Turkish series with better quality Arab productions.
Many TV channels in Uzbekistan are betting on Turkish TV series to raise ratings and expand their audience.
Turkish series draw the mind of the audience into delusional thoughts and do not have any luggage in practice, based on a vulgar culture in a romantic frame.
The space rover Perseverance will deliver to the surface of Mars a device capable of extracting oxygen from the atmosphere of the planet.
The Pereverance rover, which has been exploring Mars since February, processed carbon dioxide into oxygen with the help of an experimental MOXIE spacecraft installed aboard the rover.
The rover produced 5.4 grams of oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars.
Astronauts need an additional source of oxygen in the rocket to safely return to Earth, since the atmosphere of Mars is 96% carbon dioxide.
The converted oxygen can also be used for future human exploration of Mars.
A drone is installed on the Perseverance device to test the possibility of flying in the rarefied atmosphere of Mars.
The technology for obtaining oxygen from the Martian atmosphere will be used to obtain rocket fuel needed during manned missions to Mars.
The experimental device is called MOXIE (Mars Oxygen In-Situ Resource Utilization Experiment)
On April 20, the Pereverance rover processed carbon dioxide into oxygen with the help of an experimental MOXIE spacecraft installed aboard the rover.
The technology of extracting oxygen from the atmosphere of Mars will help astronauts produce rocket fuel on the planet.
The applicants of the lawsuits believe that the company's product - the herbicide Roundup - can cause cancer.
Bayer will pay about $9.5 billion to settle claims filed by lawyers representing some 95,000 plaintiffs.
Bayer AG reached a settlement to settle 75 percent of the 125,000 lawsuits alleging cancer caused by its top-selling herbicide Roundup.

Bayer AG will announce that it has reached an approximately $10.5 billion agreement with plaintiffs in the United States who have accused its Roundup herbicide of causing cancer to settle tens of thousands of lawsuits.
Bayer, the German chemical company, said on Wednesday that a legal dispute over cancer caused by its Roundup brand herbicide has reached a $2 billion settlement with regulators.
Monsanto has been sued in the United States for suspected cancer caused by the herbicide Roundup, and Bayer is seeking to settle the lawsuit with $11 billion in damages.
Three California juries rejected the company’s massive damages award in the Roundup lawsuit.
The company said in April it was facing a lawsuit from 100,000 plaintiffs in the United States alleging that Roundup and other glyphosate-based herbicides caused them to develop cancer.
The German company lost three lawsuits filed between 2018 and 2019 by Roundup users who said the product caused them to develop cancer.
Bayer pledged to pay $10.9 billion in court compensation for cancer patients from the herbicide Roundup.
Bayer will pay up to $9.6 billion in damages to settle an existing Roundup case related to non-Hodgkin lymphoma.
Bayer is working to resolve about 125,000 similar claims.
The herbicide Roundup was developed by the American company Monsanto, which came under the control of Bayer since June last year.
Workers from the Italian city of Piacenza found a painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a woman ”, which since 1997 was considered stolen.
The painting was painted by Klimt in 1916-1917.

In the Italian city of Piacenza, a gardener peeled ivy off the wall in the Ricci Oddi gallery and accidentally found a painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman ” stolen 22 years ago from the same gallery.
The painting, Portrait of a Lady, was stolen by thieves with a fishing line in Klimt's later years.
The painting was accidentally recovered when a gardener cleaned the ivy on the museum’s exterior.
The stolen painting “Portrait of a Lady” by Gustav Klimt was found wrapped in a black cloth bag and hidden by a metal plate in the garden wall of the Ricci Oddia Piacenza Modern Art Museum.
The painting “Portrait of a Lady” by Gustav Klimt was stolen from the Ricci Oddia Piacenza Museum of Modern Art on February 22, 1997.
Prolonged rains and low temperatures over the past three months have significantly affected the harvesting of a number of crops.
Heat exhaustion, frostbite, lack of water resources, and widespread climate change harm the ability to pursue old ways of cultivating crops.
Climate change is putting food security at risk.
Drought and climate change affect food security in Gonabad, Iran
Frost: in drought will help a cardinal change in the structure of crops
Rapid global climate change has seriously affected the growth of crops, including warming and changes in rainfall patterns.
The amount of rainfall and the type of rainfall distribution affect production of some crops.
Agricultural productivity has fallen sharply due to the effects of climate change.
Climate change has reduced rainfall.
Climate change may lead to a zone of risky farming in two-thirds of the territory of Ukraine.
The agriculture industry is already adapting to climate change.
Climate change could cause local problems with agricultural productivity in all regions.
Allocation of optimal cultivation pattern in the province is necessary due to the consequences of climate change.
The impact of climate change will further exacerbate the threats and challenges faced by the agricultural sector.
Climate change makes eating more challenging for Australian farmers.
Mohammad Rasoulof, the director of “There Is No Evil”, was prevented from receiving the award by the Iranian authorities.
Iranian cinema won the first prize at the Berlin Film Festival in 2011 with Asghar Farhadi's "The Separation".
Mohammad Rasoulof was unable to travel abroad to receive the Golden Bear Award in person.
The film "There Is No Evil", directed by Iranian dissident director Mohammad Rasoulof, won the Golden Bear Award for Best Picture at the Berlin Film Festival.
The film received the main award from the jury, which is generally well received by critics.
"Never, rarely, sometimes, always" won a Silver Bear in the jury.
Eliza Hittman's “Never Rarely Sometimes Always” won the Jury Silver Bear Award.
The “Golden Bear” is the main award of the Berlinale.
Romanian director Radu Jude and Polish director Malgorzata Szumowksa shared the award for best director for Aferimi and Body, respectively.
Iranian film Innocence wins Golden Bear at Berlin Film Festival
“There Is No Evil ”, the latest film by well-known Iranian filmmaker Mohammad Rasoulof, won the most important award at the 2020 Berlin Film Festival, the Golden Bear.
The French and British coastguards have repeatedly intercepted boats attempting to transport Asian and African migrants across the English Channel to Britain.
On December 22, French police rescued 18 people attempting to cross the English Channel in two night raids, and a French official warned Britain of an increase in illegal sea migration.
235 illegal migrants in 17 boats attempting to cross the English Channel from northern France were intercepted by British Coast Guard and Border Guard officers on Thursday.
The British and French governments have taken special measures to tackle illegal crossings, but Paris has refused to accept migrants who have crossed the border.
Attempts by migrants to cross the English Channel by boat have increased due to fears that borders could be closed after Brexit.
Refugees were removed from boats off the south-eastern coast of the UK near the coastal cities of Hastings, Deal, Dover and Paradise.
Britain detained 148 migrants trying to enter the country through the English Channel earlier this month.
British border guards and the coast guard rescued about 50 refugees who were trying to get into the country from France, sailing across the English Channel in rubber boats.
The authorities of the UK and France detained on Saturday 74 migrants who made an illegal attempt to cross the English Channel.
The British Coast Guard intercepted four small boats in territorial waters off the coast of Kent on the night of 26 December.
31 migrants, including a teenager and four children, were in distress in the English Channel and contacted emergency services for assistance.
British border guards have seized four boats with 49 migrants trying to reach England's shores on the English Channel.
French authorities intercepted 19 migrants trying to sneak into Britain in the English Channel yesterday night.
Britain and France have repeatedly said that travelling by boat across the Channel puts the lives of migrants and their companions at risk.
A total of 24 people were rescued by French maritime services.
About 50 people attempted to travel from France to the United Kingdom.
Crossing the English Channel is not only dangerous and illegal, but also completely unnecessary.
The Roc aircraft has a wingspan of about 117 meters, which is longer than an American football stadium.
The Stratolaunch aircraft first took off in the air in April 2019 and spent more than two hours in the sky over the Mojava desert.
The Stratolaunch aircraft is planned to be used as an aerial platform for launching Pegasus XL launch vehicles.
The central part of the Stratolaunch aircraft is designed to mount space rockets
The Stratolaunch aircraft is equipped with six twin-circuit Pratt & Whitney turbojet engines
The maximum mass at which the Stratolaunch aircraft can take off is about 590 tons
The Stratolaunch aircraft is designed to launch launch vehicles from an altitude of 9100 m.
The company completed the second test flight of its vehicle, which is now known as the world ’ s largest aircraft by wingspan.
The Stratolaunch aircraft has two fuselages and a wingspan of 117 meters, which is more than the wingspan of any other aircraft that has ever taken to the sky.
The test of the Stratolaunch aircraft passed successfully
The first flight tests of Stratolaunch took place over the Mojave Desert in California.
The Tour de France organizers filed an official complaint against the fan who caused the crash.
Five days ago, spectators at the Tour de France held up handwritten signs blocking the lane, causing a tragic crash.
French police have detained a woman suspected of causing a serious accident during the famous Tour de France cycling race, waving a poster on the track for athletes.
Jumbo Visma cyclist Tony Martin hit a fan's cardboard sign holding it in the middle of the road and crashed the other cyclists into the ground.
The Tour de France official tweeted that spectators should respect the safety of athletes and not pose a danger for taking photos or snatching TV footage.
One of the victims could not continue to participate in the first stage of the Tour de France.
45 km before the end of the first stage of the legendary cycling race "Tour de France" there was a massive blockage, as a result of which more than two dozen athletes were injured.
The cyclist made a hard fall after one of the cheerleaders crossed the barrier line to show his poster to the camera.
Crew Dragon is the first American manned spacecraft launched to the ISS since the completion of the Space Shuttle program in 2011, and the first ever manned spacecraft built by a private company.
Crew Dragon will be used in the near future to send NASA astronauts to the International Space Station and other long-term space missions.
The Crew Dragon spacecraft made its first successful manned flight in May, bringing astronauts Robert Benken and Douglas Hurley to the International Space Station.
The Dragon successfully docked with the International Space Station at about 10 pm local time on Sunday (31st), nineteen hours after launch.
SpaceX and NASA on May 30 on the second attempt sent manned by two astronauts spacecraft Crew Dragon to the International Space Station.

The manned spacecraft Crew Dragon of the American company SpaceX with a crew of four, launched on April 23 with the help of the carrier rocket Falcon 9, successfully docked to the International Space Station (ISS) a day after launch.
The Crew Dragon spacecraft, provided and operated by SpaceX, docked with the International Space Station at 422 kilometers above China.
American and Japanese astronauts moved from SpaceX ’ s Crew Dragon spacecraft to the International Space Station.
The Crew Dragon spacecraft will launch from the United States on May 27.
SpaceX launches manned Crew Dragon-2 to the ISS.
The press service of the North American Aerospace Agency (NASA) reported the launch of the Crew Dragon-2 spacecraft.
Elon Musk sent to the ISS the ship Crew Dragon with four astronauts on board
SpaceX ’ s Crew Dragon spacecraft docked with the International Space Station for the first time last weekend.
The Supreme Court of the Canadian province of Ontario ruled that Iran should compensate families who sued for the plane crash that killed 176 people
The court granted the claim of four families against Iran, whose relatives died in the plane crash.

President of Ukraine Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that lawsuits will be filed against Iran in international courts if this country does not adhere to its obligations in connection with the crash of the UIA aircraft.
Iran's killing of Ukraine Flight 752 was a terrorist act, according to a Canadian court ruling.
Some relatives of the dead passengers of the UIA flight PS752, shot down by the Iranian military, filed a lawsuit against Iran in the Federal Court in Washington.
Canadian lawyers for the victims of Bahman 752 flight filed a lawsuit in Canadian courts for $1.5 billion in damages.

Families of the victims of Flight 752 inside Iran gathered in front of the Judiciary Organization of the Armed Forces on May 17 to demand an indictment against three senior Iranian security and military officials.
A Canadian court has no jurisdiction to hear a claim for damages in the crash of a Ukrainian passenger plane shot down by Islamic Revolutionary Guards
The judge ruled in absentia against Iran on non-fulfillment of obligations in a civil lawsuit filed by four families of the victims on board the UIA flight
The Ontario Superior Court of Justice in Canada determined that the plane was hit by two missiles shortly after take-off in Tehran as a result of a deliberate act.
Four survivors of the victims of Flight 752 have publicly filed a lawsuit against Iran in Ontario, along with a woman who does not want to be named.

The judge of the court emphasized that based on available evidence, the IRGC ’ s missile attacks on Ukrainian International Flight PS752 in the winter of 1398 were intentional and directly caused the death of all the passengers of the aircraft.
Iran shot down the Ukrainian plane with flight number PS-752 on January 8, 2019, using at least two IRGC defense missiles, resulting in the death of all 176 people on board.
Russia's embassy in North Korea announced that North Korea had detained a Russian fishing boat and detained 15 Russian and two South Korean crew members on board for illegal entry.
The reason for North Korea’s capture of the ship was a violation of the country’s water borders by Russian fishermen
The Russian fishing vessel was released on the 27th, and took all the crew to Sokcho Port, Gangwon Province, South Korea, around 1: 00 p.m. on the 28th.
The Russian fishing vessel, Xiang Hai Lin 8, departed from South Korea’s Sokcho port for the port of Zarubino in Russia on the afternoon of the 16th.
The South Korean government sent letters to North Korea several times after the incident, asking for the return of two South Korean citizens.
The 15 Russian sailors and two South Korean crew members aboard the boat were detained by the North Korean coast guard and held in a hotel in Wonsan.
South Korea's unity ministry said the ship was diverted from its route and entered North Korean waters due to a technical engine problem.
The Russian Embassy in North Korea told the TASS news agency that the North Korean border guard team detained the Russian fishing boat at 4:30 am on the 17th.
The vessel is owned by the Northeast Fishing Company.
Pyongyang is currently investigating the Xiang Hai Lin 8 and is expected to return the fishing boat and its crew to Russia after the investigation.
The fishing company that owns the boat denied the charge of intruding into North Korea's waters.
South Korea is keeping the North Korean and Russian authorities informed of the incident.
The Japanese government will resume commercial whaling in restricted areas such as offshore Japan in July next year.
Japan stopped commercial whaling in 1988, but continued to hunt about 200 to 1,200 whales a year in Antarctica and the Pacific Northwest in the name of scientific research.
Japan to withdraw from International Whaling Commission in order to resume commercial whaling
Japan has been criticized by environmental organizations, primarily Australian and New Zealand, for catching and killing whales in the past 10 years.
The Japanese government claims it will conduct commercial whaling based on scientific data without affecting the continuation of whale species.

Japan on Sunday (June 30) formally withdrew from the International Whaling Commission (IWC), resuming commercial whaling for the first time in more than 30 years and setting an upper limit of 227 whaling by the end of 2019.
A small fleet of ships will begin commercial whaling from the port of Shimonoseki in western Japan on July 1.
A proposal by Japan to restart commercial whaling was rejected at the International Whaling Commission meeting in September.
In 1982, the IWC decided to suspend commercial whaling when some whale species faced extinction.
There are plans to catch 383 whales in the coming year, and 227 by the end of December this year.
Ernest Hemingway's best work comes from personal tensions and struggles.
Ernest Hemingway wrote from real, live experiences.
Ernest Hemingway was born in the United States and was part of the "Lost Generation", a group of young people who lost opportunities due to the First World War.
Ernest Hemingway fought in Italy, lived in Cuba, and traveled to Africa.
Ernest Hemingway can write simple text without needing a dictionary.
Ernest Hemingway presented one of the most fascinating stories of the 20th century in "The Old Man and the Sea".
Ernest Hemingway explained his style with the metaphor of an "iceberg" in his Nobel Prize speech.
Ernest Hemingway's participation in the war influenced his literary work and gave him a "war-torn" temperament.
Ernest Hemingway did not consider himself a literary and literary writer in the classical sense.
Ernest Hemingway wrote a beautiful and moving story called "The Snows of Kilimanjaro" in the 1950s.
Ernest Hemingway ended his life 50 years ago.
Ernest Hemingway influenced many writers after World War II.
Ernest Miller Hemingway discussed the effects of loneliness on people.
Ernest Hemingway's novel The Old Man and the Sea, written in 1952, won the Pulitzer Prize in 1953 and the Nobel Prize a year later.
Ernest Hemingway cannot be considered a "committed" writer, despite being "engaged" with political adventures all his life.
Ernest Hemingway changed the volume 47 times for "war dream" At the end of "To Arms".

Ernest Hemingway was an adventurer for most of his life, from being wounded in Italy by 200 pieces of Austrian shrapnel during World War I, to taking part in the frontlines of the Spanish Civil War or touring the African wilderness, to fishing and hunting wildlife and living in Cuba.
Ernest Hemingway is one of the emblems of the modern world.
The task of the ITER project is to demonstrate the scientific and technological feasibility of using thermonuclear energy on an industrial scale
China, the European Union, India, Japan, South Korea, Russia and the United States are participating in the work on ITER
ITER will become the world's first power station level fusion experimental reactor, which is an indispensable step to finally realize the commercialization of magnetic confinement fusion.
An international experimental thermonuclear reactor ITER is being built in France
China is accelerating its own pace of development, with the goal of designing a fully fledged fusion reactor capable of generating electricity.
ITER originally planned to be a 30-year plan, but according to the current progress, experimental results will not be available until 2025.
ITER is an international project on which more than 30 countries are working.
The International Thermonuclear Fusion Experimental Reactor (ITER) is humanity’s largest nuclear fusion project.
ITER is a project of an international experimental thermonuclear reactor based on the tokamak installation laid down in 2005.
The assembly of the experimental thermonuclear reactor ITER is a grandiose international project comparable in scale with the ISS or the Large Hadron Collider.
